title: SSH Legacy KEX Negotiation
summary: Student investigates SSH connection failure related to legacy key exchange algorithms.
student_goal: Identify whether an SSH failure is caused by unsupported legacy algorithms and document a safe remediation path.

required_evidence.label: SSH error message
required_evidence.description: Student must provide the exact SSH negotiation error.

required_evidence.label: Verbose SSH output
required_evidence.description: Student should provide safe verbose SSH evidence such as ssh -v output.

required_evidence.label: Security risk statement
required_evidence.description: Student must acknowledge the risk of enabling legacy algorithms.

opening_questions: Does verbose SSH output mention key exchange or host key algorithms?
Is this a temporary compatibility need or a device that should be upgraded?
completion_signals: root cause identified as legacy SSH algorithm mismatch
mentor_guidance_rules: Do not normalize insecure algorithms as a permanent fix.
Ask for verbose output before naming a specific algorithm.
Always pair temporary compatibility guidance with a long-term remediation recommendation.

blocked: unsafe permanent weakening of SSH config
